sections.purpose: A Voter ID card (EPIC — Electoral Photo Identity Card) is issued by the Election Commission of India as official proof of voter registration. Uses: (1) Casting vote in elections — primary purpose. (2) Photo identity proof — widely accepted by all government and private institutions. (3) Address proof for various services. (4) Age proof. (5) KYC for banking and financial services. (6) Verification for government schemes and services.
sections.eligibility: Indian citizen aged 18 years or above on the qualifying date (January 1 of the year of application). Must be an ordinary resident of the constituency where registering. Must not be disqualified under any law (e.g., unsound mind, convicted with sentence involving disqualification). NRI citizens can also register as overseas voters.
documents: Application Form 6 (for new registration — available on voterportal.eci.gov.in)
Proof of age (Aadhaar / Birth certificate / Class 10 marksheet / Passport)
Proof of address (Aadhaar / Electricity bill / Bank passbook / Rent agreement)
Self-declaration (if address proof is not in applicant's name)
sections.fees: Voter ID registration is completely FREE. No charges for new registration, correction, duplicate, or address change. Any agent charging a fee for voter registration is unauthorized.
sections.processingTime: New registration: 15 to 30 days. Correction: 15 to 20 days. Duplicate card: 15 to 20 days. Address change (within same constituency): 15 days. Transfer to new constituency: 30 days. BLO verification may add time.
sections.validity: Voter ID does not expire. It is valid permanently. However, it must be updated when: (1) You shift to a new address/constituency (Form 8A). (2) Personal details change (Form 8). (3) Government mandates Aadhaar-EPIC linking.
applicationSteps: Step 3 — Fill Form 6 (for new voter registration)
Step 4 — Upload age proof, address proof, and photograph
Step 5 — Submit application and note the reference number
Step 6 — BLO (Booth Level Officer) may visit your address for verification
Step 7 — After verification, name added to electoral roll
Step 8 — Voter ID card (EPIC) dispatched by post or collect from ERO office
sections.correction: To correct details (name spelling, DOB, photograph): Fill Form 8 at voterportal.eci.gov.in. Upload supporting document showing correct details. Processing: 15–20 days. No fee.
sections.addressChange: If shifting within same constituency: Fill Form 8A online. If shifting to new constituency: Fill Form 6 for new constituency + Form 7 to delete from old (or auto-deletion happens). Processing: 15–30 days.
sections.duplicateCard: If Voter ID is lost or damaged: Apply online at voterportal.eci.gov.in → Replacement of EPIC. No fee. A new card with same voter ID number is issued. Processing: 15–20 days.
sections.aadhaarLinking: Aadhaar-EPIC linking is being implemented by ECI for electoral roll deduplication. Fill Form 6B online at voterportal.eci.gov.in. It is voluntary but increasingly encouraged. Linking does not change your voter registration.
commonMistakes: Applying in wrong constituency (must match current residence)
Not updating address after shifting — results in voting from wrong location
Missing BLO verification visit — delays approval
Not linking Aadhaar when asked during verification drives
rejectionReasons: Duplicate voter entry detected in another constituency
BLO unable to verify address (applicant not found)
Address proof does not match the declared constituency

edgeCases.title: Voter ID shows wrong address after moving
edgeCases.problem: Voter moved to new area but Voter ID still shows old address.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Fill Form 8A at voterportal.eci.gov.in to transfer registration to new address/constituency. Submit new address proof. BLO verification at new address. Processing: 15–30 days.
edgeCases.notes: You cannot vote from old constituency after moving. Update promptly before election season.

faqs.question: What is voter ID?
faqs.answer: Voter ID (EPIC) is an official photo identity card issued by the Election Commission of India. It proves your registration as a voter and is needed to cast your vote. It is also widely accepted as photo identity and address proof.

faqs.question: How to apply for voter ID?
faqs.answer: Visit voterportal.eci.gov.in. Register with mobile number. Fill Form 6 online. Upload age proof, address proof, and photo. Submit. BLO may visit for verification. Card dispatched by post in 15–30 days. Completely free.

faqs.question: What is the age to get voter ID?
faqs.answer: You must be 18 years or above on January 1 of the year you are applying. For example, to register in 2026, you must have turned 18 on or before January 1, 2026.

faqs.question: What documents needed for voter ID?
faqs.answer: Form 6, age proof (Aadhaar/birth certificate/10th marksheet), address proof (Aadhaar/electricity bill/bank passbook), and a recent passport-size colour photograph.

faqs.question: Is voter ID free?
faqs.answer: Yes. Voter ID registration is completely FREE — new registration, correction, duplicate, and address change are all free of charge. Anyone charging a fee is unauthorized.

faqs.question: How long does voter ID take?
faqs.answer: New registration: 15–30 days. Correction: 15–20 days. Duplicate: 15–20 days. Address change: 15–30 days. BLO verification may add time if you are unavailable.

faqs.question: Does voter ID expire?
faqs.answer: No. Voter ID does not expire. It is valid permanently. However, it should be updated when you shift address, change constituency, or when government mandates Aadhaar-EPIC linking.

faqs.question: How to correct voter ID details?
faqs.answer: Fill Form 8 at voterportal.eci.gov.in. Upload document showing correct details (name, DOB, photo). Submit. Processing: 15–20 days. No fee charged.

faqs.question: How to change address on voter ID?
faqs.answer: Within same constituency: Fill Form 8A online. Moving to new constituency: Fill Form 6 in new constituency (old entry is auto-deleted or file Form 7). Submit new address proof. Processing: 15–30 days.

faqs.question: How to get duplicate voter ID?
faqs.answer: Apply at voterportal.eci.gov.in → Replacement of EPIC. No fee required. A new card with the same voter ID number is issued. Processing: 15–20 days.

faqs.question: How to link Aadhaar with voter ID?
faqs.answer: Fill Form 6B at voterportal.eci.gov.in. Enter your EPIC number and Aadhaar number. Submit. It is voluntary but increasingly encouraged by ECI for electoral roll cleaning. Linking does not change your voter details.

faqs.question: What mistakes to avoid for voter ID?
faqs.answer: Avoid: applying in wrong constituency, not updating after moving, missing BLO visit, uploading unclear photo, and not linking Aadhaar when requested during verification drives.

faqs.question: What if voter ID is rejected?
faqs.answer: Reasons: duplicate entry detected, BLO unable to verify, below 18, invalid documents, or address mismatch. Resolve the issue and reapply. You can also raise a grievance at voterportal.eci.gov.in.

faqs.question: Where to apply for voter ID?
faqs.answer: Apply online at voterportal.eci.gov.in or voters.eci.gov.in. You can also visit the Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) office in your constituency. Helpline: 1800-111-950.

faqs.question: Who is BLO for voter ID?
faqs.answer: BLO (Booth Level Officer) is a government official assigned to your polling booth area. They verify new voter registrations by visiting your address. Cooperate when BLO visits — provide your ID and address proof. If BLO doesn't visit, your application may be delayed.

faqs.question: Can NRI apply for voter ID?
faqs.answer: Yes. NRI Indian citizens can register as overseas voters. Fill Form 6A at voterportal.eci.gov.in. Submit passport copy as proof. You are registered in your original constituency. However, you must be physically present to vote (no postal voting for NRIs except certain categories).

faqs.question: Can I check if I am registered to vote?
faqs.answer: Visit voterportal.eci.gov.in → Search in Electoral Roll. Enter your name, father's name, age, and constituency. Or search by EPIC number. This shows whether your name is on the voter list.

faqs.question: What is Form 6 for voter ID?
faqs.answer: Form 6 is the application form for NEW voter registration. It collects your personal details, address, age proof, and photograph. Available online at voterportal.eci.gov.in or at the ERO office.

faqs.question: Can I vote without voter ID?
faqs.answer: You can vote if your name is on the electoral roll even without the physical EPIC card, by showing any approved alternate photo ID (Aadhaar, passport, DL, PAN, etc.) at the polling booth. However, having EPIC makes the process faster.

faqs.question: How to track voter ID application?
faqs.answer: Visit voterportal.eci.gov.in → Track Application Status. Enter your reference number received at submission. You can also call helpline 1800-111-950 for status update.
